question_text: Find the slope of segment AB shown below.

answer_key.explanation: The slope is 2. Slope is rise over run — change in y divided by change in x. Here y goes from 2 to 8 (rise = 6) and x goes from 1 to 4 (run = 3); 6/3 = 2. This is the same idea as the gradient of any straight line in coordinate geometry.
common_mistakes: Students often invert run and rise; this leads to 1/2 instead of 2 because they divide change in x by change in y.

visual.caption: Segment AB on the coordinate plane.
visual.altText: Coordinate plane with point A at (1,2) and point B at (4,8) joined by a segment.
